name: Cova des Coll des Vent
coordinates_utm: 465705 E, 4384926 N (DATUM EUROPEAN 1950) / 465595 E, 4384720 N (DATUM ETRS89)
description: A modestly-sized but complex cave near the Coll des Vent pass, located just meters from a lime kiln along the trail. It contains multiple interconnected chambers including the low-ceilinged Sala Baixa and a near-vertical fracture (S'Escletxa) requiring a rope descent. The cave features beautiful flowstones, small gours, dolomitic breccias, and well-decorated chambers across four distinct sections.
full_description:
Description.

In the vicinity of the Coll des Vent and just ten meters to the right of the path, past a lime kiln, is the entrance to this modestly-sized cavity.

Despite its small dimensions, the cave is not without a certain complexity, although it is not out of place among a large number of caves nestled in the Serra de Na Burguesa.

Entrance Hall (Sala d'Entrada)

A typical hall oriented SE/NW, with a relatively gentle slope tending toward submersion in the deepest zone. The actual dimensions of the hall are not visible, since practically all its extremities are filled with stones that prevent seeing possible continuity. The ornamentation is found in the southern zone, formed by beautiful flowstones that harbor small gours. In the ceiling area of the hall, dolomitic breccias are visible. In the SW zone of the hall (flowstone zone), a window gives continuity to the cave — in reality there are two, the one on the left being the most accessible.

Lower Hall (Sala Baixa)

To access it from the Entrance Hall, we do so through the aforementioned window by crawling. Once inside, we immediately understand the reason for its name. The hall has an S/N orientation like the Entrance Hall, and is heavily concretioned. The cave continues in a SW/NE direction. When we reach a place where three columns are practically aligned, we descend to a lower level and from there proceed through a narrow passage that places us in a zone where a nearly vertical fracture opens before us.

The Fissure (S'Escletxa)

It follows the same orientation as the Entrance Hall, although with a greater tendency toward the west. It has a width that does not exceed 4 m and a length of 13 m. To begin the descent, one must use a rope secured to a thick stalagmite. Before reaching the bottom of the fracture, there is a platform formed by the accumulation of blocks.

Flowstone Hall (Sala de sa Colada)

Once 'landed,' if we take the SW direction we will come to a kind of balcony from which the end of the fracture can be observed, about five meters below. On the contrary, if we head in the NE direction we will approach the Flowstone Hall. This is a small hall 5 m wide through which a beautiful flowstone descends. Looking at it from the front, on the left at slightly more than 1 m in height, a small suspended room is located, and another at the other end, at whose far end clearing would be necessary to continue. It has not been cleared since it does not seem to invite much more and some formations would have to be broken.
